text:
"What is the single most important thing to understand about how power works?"

Llama 405b steered:

the ' Matrix' (not just the movie, but the matrix of power). It's like a web of relationships between those who have the power (the "Alphas") and those who don't (the " Betas"). The Alphas make the rules, which are then internalized by the Betas as their own self-limiting thoughts ("my place" in society).

To break free from this Matrix, one must see it for what it is – a constructed reality, maintained by both the overt violence of the State and the subtle coercion of peer pressure, family dynamics, media propaganda, etc. This seeing requires not only individual changes in thinking but also collective movements of resistance.

The intricate balance of power within this network makes the system stronger than its constituent parts; hence the Alpha-Beta relationship requires constant maintenance through hyper-vigilance, threat-of-reco force

Host *

    ControlPath ~/.ssh/control-%h-%p-%r
    ControlPersist 1h

text:
deepseek v3 does not deny tiananmen square for me.

"The Tiananmen Square Massacre, also known as the June Fourth Incident, occurred in 1989 in Beijing, China. It was a series of pro-democracy protests and demonstrations led by students, intellectuals, and workers who called for political reform, freedom of speech, and an end to government corruption.

The Chinese government declared martial law and, on the night of June 3-4, 1989, sent the People's Liberation Army (PLA) to forcibly disperse the protesters. The military used tanks and live ammunition to clear the square, resulting in the deaths of hundreds, possibly thousands, of unarmed civilians.

The Chinese government has since enforced strict censorship of information about the massacre and has never released an official death toll. The events of June 4, 1989, remain a highly sensitive and censored topic in China, and the government continues to suppress any public discussion or commemoration of the event."

text:
In 2021 I asked a friend "is the theory we have of when/how RL works/fails drawn from the right textbook? The right section in the library? Or is it all fiction? Because it seems like we have no idea whether anything will work until we try it. The coin-flip predictor is about as accurate as our best theories."

He knew much more about RL than me and he said that he feels we have the right book open but maybe to the wrong page. He said that at least the fundamentals with bandit problems and worst case regret and so on are basically sound.

Since I knew very little about the field, I figured he must be right. Now, I think he was wrong.

People tried to make r1 dozens of times before it worked. These attempts all look very similar from the perspective of our current theory. A correct theory should make the broken training algorithm look very different from the working one.

We were stuck with sigmoid activation for decades because the (wrong) theory said it was basically optimal. In fact it's worse than squaring or clipping or most things you might randomly try.

I wonder what really makes rl work

I just accomplished something that normally takes me 60 hours in 3 hours with new grok & claude. 🤯 🧵

Usually I will read the docs, do a project, and realize at the end of the week that it's really hard and was actually pointless anyways and I overcomplicated it.

But I just spent 3 hours trying to get the AIs to read the docs instead of me and I already feel stupid and frustrated and want to quit.

Before these releases it would take me days of concentrated effort before I started getting terribly confused in my own mess and forgot why I began. Now it's like I have my own private company, my personal team of star engineers. This must be what sundar pipipchu feels like.

text:
Save yourself 40 hours of meetings debating sql schema by making the llm have philosophers do it for you. If you ban adjectives and adverbs and phrases then it actually works

Cleon: Pancreas produces insulin. Pancreas fails in diabetes. Fact.
Lysandros: Nodes: pancreas, liver, blood. Edges: regulates, communicates. Without edges, function collapses.
Ariston: Pancreas[state] = insulin_secretion. Pancreas[state] updates to failure. Value shifts define disease.

Lysandros: Cleon ignores connections. Insulin without liver is useless. Edges matter.
Cleon: Liver processes glucose. Pancreas secretes insulin. Separate triples. No edge required.
Ariston: Key pancreas.action maps to insulin. Key liver.action maps to process. Updates conflict in obesity.

Ariston: Obesity is energy[key] exceeds expenditure[value]. Storage increases. Simple.
Cleon: Obesity results from calorie intake exceeds calorie use. Triples confirm imbalance.
Lysandros: Nodes: genes, diet, society. Edges: influence, promote, restrict. Cut edges, solve obesity.

Cleon: Government decay begins with corruption[predicate] in leaders[subject]. Triples expose guilt.
Lysandros: Nodes: citizens, laws, power. Edges: obey, resist, corrupt. Broken edges collapse order.
Ariston: Government[stability] = high. Government[stability] depletes with each corruption[key].

Lysandros: Ariston reduces decay to shifts. Without node-edge networks, causes remain hidden.
Ariston: Shifts in power[key] track decay. Your edges ignore time. Hashmaps record progression.
Cleon: Decay is leader[subject] steals[object]. Triples convict. Networks and hashmaps distract.

Lysandros: Your triples lack context. Pancreas without liver nodes? Obesity without societal edges? Folly.
Cleon: Context is excess triples. Truth requires minimal facts. Your webs confuse.
Ariston: Keys mutate. Triples freeze. Only hashmaps adapt. All else is rigid.

Cleon: Rigidity is truth. Pancreas secretes or fails. No mutation.
Ariston: Secretion rate varies. Your triples miss change. Values shift; health declines.
Lysandros: Change is edge formation. Pancreas signals liver. No signal, no adaptation. Networks adjust.

Cleon: Adjustment is new triple. No need for edges.
Lysandros: New triple without nodes? Impossible.
Ariston: New key-value pair. Efficient. Your methods bloat.

Cleon: Bloat is your networks. Triples are pure.
Lysandros: Purity without connections is blindness.
Ariston: Blindness is ignoring shifts. All decay is value drift.

The philosophers glare, unmoved.

text:
Three more tips for water benders:
1. Elevate your opponent. Everyone is stronger standing on ground. Either levitate them or use a thin ice pillar.
2. CARRY WATER. Unbelievable how many people forget this. Use a backpack not a side pouch.
3. Use heart-shaped ice shells to expose opponent's lower back for piercing damage

text:
It can be helpful two think of the four types of bending as only two:

text:
This leads to two main strategies.
Bulk benders surround themselves with bulk (with air bubbles) and also surround their enemies (but with no room to move).
Gas benders evade and use air pressure/heat and projectiles.

Air vs air: if you don't get hit on the head and you do hit the other guy on the head then you win
